key_concerns: Grade 3/4 neutropenia and thrombocytopenia requiring dose reduction in 49% of patients
Potential for severe bleeding and infections, though comparable to placebo
IRRs, LFT abnormalities, and hepatic adverse events, mostly non-severe
key_positives: Statistically significant improvement in ≥8-week and ≥24-week RBC TI (p<0.001)
Durable transfusion independence with median 51.6 weeks for ≥8-week TI responders
17.8% of patients achieved ≥1-year RBC TI, clinically meaningful in this population
Exposure-efficacy positive relationship supports dose selection
Unmet medical need with limited approved therapies for ESA-refractory LR MDS
Safety profile manageable with dose modifications and monitoring

evidence_notes: "≥ 8-week RBC TI: 39.8% in the imetelstat group compared to 15.0% in the placebo group, p < 0.001" - Primary endpoint met with large effect size.
"≥ 24-week RBC TI: 28.0% in the imetelstat group compared to 3.3% in the placebo group, p < 0.001" - Durable response key secondary endpoint.
"Among patients who achieved ≥ 8-week RBC TI, the median length of TI was 51.6 weeks in imetelstat arm and 13.3 weeks in placebo" - Demonstrates long duration of benefit.
"17.8% of patients in the imetelstat group had ≥ 1-year RBC TI, versus 1.7% in the placebo group (p=0.002)" - One-third of life expectancy in TI, highly clinically meaningful.
"no significant E-R relationship for any grade or Grade 3/4 infection or bleeding events" - Suggests cytopenias not translating into higher severe clinical events.
"Dose reductions mitigated the risks of clinical consequences... while enabling patients to continue treatment" - Risk management strategy effective.

Positives: Statistically significant improvement in ≥8-week and ≥24-week RBC TI (p<0.001); Durable transfusion independence with median 51.6 weeks for ≥8-week TI responders; 17.8% of patients achieved ≥1-year RBC TI, clinically meaningful in this population; Exposure-efficacy positive relationship supports dose selection; Unmet medical need with limited approved therapies for ESA-refractory LR MDS
